$comment: Example hook wiring for CHAOS artifact provenance metadata (see ../README.md and reference/artifact-metadata-config.md). This file is illustrative only and is not read directly by Claude Code. Copy the "hooks" object from whichever profile you want into your own .claude/settings.json or .claude/settings.local.json. Neither profile stamps files automatically -- both are report-only with respect to writes; only --check-only is wired here. Add --stamp yourself (with --dry-run first) if you want a hook to also insert/repair metadata, and understand that this then writes to disk during a turn.

contributorSafe.description: Recommended default. Warns on missing/invalid chaosMetadata but never blocks a turn and never writes files automatically.

strict.description: Blocking enforcement. Exits 2 (blocking, per Claude Code hook conventions) when a CHAOS-managed Markdown file is missing or has invalid chaosMetadata, per policies.artifactMetadata.failOnMissingMetadataInStrictHooks.
